sections.heading: Education
items: Ph.D. in Mass Communication, Loughborough University, UK (2018)
M.A. in Journalism, National Taiwan University, Taiwan (2008)

sections.heading: Experiences
items: 2021.8–present: Associate Professor, Graduate Institute of Journalism, National Taiwan University
2021.8–2022.7: Project-Appointed Assistant Professor, Department of Adult and Continuing Education, National Taiwan Normal University
2018.8–2021.7: Assistant Professor, Department of Mass Communication, Tamkang University

sections.heading: Journal Articles
items: Meng, J., Lin, Y.-P., & Tsai, H.-J. (2024). Environmental activism in the platform society: Spatial agency in digital maps. New Media & Society, 26(12), 7058–7080.

Tsai, H.-J. (2023). Data activism: Initiatives, practices, and connections among NGO workers and open-source communities in a platformized civil society. Communication and Society, 64, 29–62. [In Chinese]
Tsai, H.-J. (2022). Platform capitalism: Spectacle and crisis of platformized cultural industries. Taiwan: A Radical Quarterly in Social Studies, 121, 209–217. [In Chinese]

Tsai, H.-J., & Lin, Y.-P. (2021). Precarious media labor in the era of post-COVID-19: Rethinking the supporting policy for the film and TV industry globally and in Taiwan. Mass Communication Research, 148, 1–46. [In Chinese]
Tsai, H. (2021). Contextualizing the phenomenon of Big Data: A looming crisis of monopolies in the global media & Big Tech. Taiwan Journal of East Asian Studies, 18(1), 89–130. [In Chinese]

Tsai, H. (2020). Audience commodity in the age of streaming services: An analysis of Netflix binge-watching and the associated audience labor through critical political economy of communications. Chinese Journal of Communication Research, 37, 83–112. [In Chinese]

sections.heading: Book Chapters
items: Tsai, H. (2022). Critical visual literacy in the digital media era. In W.-C. Wang, Y.-P. Lin & L.-J. Wang (Eds.), Digital Communication Literacy Education in the AI Era (pp. 233–255). Wunan, Taipei.

Tsai, H. (2021). Towards critical data research: Rethinking information and platform monopolies. In C.-D. Liu & C.-S. Feng (Eds.), Communication is Understanding: A Critical Analysis of Contemporary Cross-Strait Media Issues (pp. 233–255). Taipei: NCCU COM Research & Development Center.

Lin, Y.-P., & Tsai, H.-J. (2020). Indie music as cool ambassadors? The international dimension of music policy in Taiwan, 2010–2017. In E. Tsai, T. Ho & M. Jian (Eds.), Made in Taiwan: Studies in Popular Music (pp. 171–188). Routledge.

Tsai, H.-J., & Lin, Y.-P. (2018). Neoliberalised development of cultural policies in Taiwan and a case of the Taiwanese film industry in a creative industries model. In V. Durrer, T. Miller & D. O'Brien (Eds.), The Routledge Handbook of Global Cultural Policy (pp. 449–467). London: Routledge.

Tsai, H. (2016). Fighting the neoliberalised media market & state interference: The interdependency of the Taiwan PTS and civil society organisations. In Lowe & Yamamoto (Eds.), Public Service Media Across Borders and Boundaries, RIPE@2015 (pp. 121–135). Goteborg: Nordicom.
